summary: Updated `src/styles/global.css`: replaced `--bg-nav` token from dark navy `rgba(15, 25, 35, 0.96)` to deep crimson `rgba(139, 26, 42, 0.96)` in all three CSS blocks (`:root` dark default, `[data-theme="light"]`, and OS fallback `@media (prefers-color-scheme: light) :root:not([data-theme])`).

Updated `src/styles/nav.css` with three colour replacements: (1) `.nav.scrolled` / `.nav.nav-force-dark` background changed from hardcoded `rgba(15, 25, 35, 0.96)` to `var(--bg-nav)` — now uses the design token; (2) `.nav-overlay` background changed from `#0f1923` to `#8B1A2A` (solid crimson); (3) Mobile `@media (max-width: 768px)` `.nav` background changed from `rgba(15, 25, 35, 0.85)` to `rgba(139, 26, 42, 0.90)` (semi-transparent crimson).
No JavaScript, Astro component structure, i18n keys, or build configuration changes were required — this was a pure CSS update.
Build validated: `./node_modules/.bin/astro build` completed successfully with 7 pages built, zero errors.
No hardcoded navy values (`#0f1923`, `rgba(15, 25, 35`) remain in `nav.css`.
`--bg-nav` token is now identical (`rgba(139, 26, 42, 0.96)`) across all three blocks in `global.css`.
